description: Administrators, lawmakers, diplomats, and civic officials.
jobs: Inquisitor
A cold-eyed interrogator rooting out heresy with questions, warrants, and heated iron.
Chancellor
A seal-stamping advisor counseling the monarch on policy and pressing wax on royal decrees.
Steward
A ledger-bowed manager balancing the estate's harvests, rents, and household spending.
Chamberlain
A robed overseer of the monarch's private quarters, wardrobe, and court ceremonies.
Seneschal
A keys-at-belt administrator running a royal household when the lord is away.
Constable
A garrison commander enforcing military discipline from the castle's highest tower.
Treasurer
A strongbox-guarding counter of crown gold, tracking debts and authorizing expenditures.
Exchequer
A checkered-cloth auditor tallying royal revenues and balancing the kingdom's accounts.
Ambassador
A foreign-court resident negotiating treaties and reporting intelligence back to the crown.
Diplomat
A travel-worn negotiator hammering out trade deals and alliances between rival nations.
Envoy
A sealed-letter carrier riding between distant courts with urgent proposals.
Herald
A tabard-wearing announcer of royal proclamations, reading decrees and identifying noble arms.
Bailiff
A writ-serving collector of rents who hauls debtors before the local court.
Reeve
A village-appointed keeper of order, assigning work and settling minor disputes.
Sheriff
A badge-wearing enforcer hunting outlaws and keeping the king's peace across a county.
Magistrate
A bench-sitting judge hearing petty offenses and market disputes with weary patience.
Judge
A wig-wearing arbiter weighing evidence and passing sentence in the high court.
Justiciar
A crown-appointed authority administering the realm's justice as chief judicial officer.
Coroner
A body-examining investigator questioning witnesses to determine cause of suspicious deaths.
Notary
A seal-pressing witness certifying documents and signatures as legally binding.
Clerk
An ink-stained copyist of letters, filing records and keeping parchment stocked.
Secretary
A quill-ready drafter of correspondence, organizing the lord's daily schedule of meetings.
Advisor
A shadow-close counselor whispering guidance into a ruler's ear on war, coin, or succession.
Counselor
A seasoned voice offering hard-won wisdom on matters of law, trade, and diplomacy.
Vizier
A turbaned power-wielder serving as the sultan's right hand in Eastern courts.
Minister
A department-heading official overseeing roads, revenue, or war for the crown.
Prefect
A district-governing enforcer collecting taxes and issuing edicts on the capital's behalf.
Governor
A near-royal authority ruling a distant province far from the capital's oversight.
Mayor
A chain-of-office wearer presiding over the town council and managing civic affairs.
Alderman
A council-seated elder voting on trade laws and civic spending in the town hall.
Tax Collector
A door-knocking tallier with a ledger, counting what each household owes the crown.
Customs Officer
A cargo-inspecting officer levying duties on imported goods at ports and borders.
Toll Keeper
A gate-staffing collector taking passage fees from every traveler crossing the bridge.
Warden
A boundary-walking guardian of a specific forest, tower, or border march.
Castellan
A garrison-commanding keeper holding a castle in the lord's absence.
Seneschalty
A multi-estate manager coordinating stewards and collecting rents across scattered holdings.
Provost
A discipline-enforcing authority maintaining order in a military camp or university.
Market Supervisor
A stall-patrolling inspector checking weights, prices, and freshness of market goods.
Weigher
A scale-calibrating official testing measures at the market to ensure honest trade.
Toll Collector
A tollbooth-sitting counter of coins as carts and riders file through the gate.
Clerk of Works
A blueprint-carrying overseer ordering stone and timber for royal building projects.
Lawyer
A statute-citing arguer of a client's case before magistrates using law and precedent.
Attorney
A writ-filing agent acting on a client's behalf and representing them in court.
Advocate
A robe-clad defender pleading the accused's case before judge and jury in open court.
Barrister
A wig-and-robe-wearing orator arguing cases in the kingdom's highest courts.
Solicitor
A contract-drafting preparer of deeds, wills, and the paperwork side of the law.
Legal Clerk
A careful-scripted copier of legal documents and filer of court records.
Executioner
A hooded figure swinging the axe that carries out the court's final sentence.
Hangman
A rope-testing professional who ties the noose and drops the condemned at dawn.
Headsman
A one-stroke specialist taking heads from shoulders with a heavy, well-honed axe.
Torturer
A iron-heating extractor of confessions, turning the rack in the dungeon's depths.
Jailer
A key-rattling keeper locking cells and sliding meals through bars day after day.
Prison Keeper
A warden managing guards, supplies, and prisoner transfers across an entire jail.
Mediator
A table-sitting neutral guiding feuding parties toward a workable compromise.
Negotiator
A concession-trading dealmaker hammering out agreements between entrenched rivals.
Peacemaker
A road-weary diplomat brokering truces in conflict zones to prevent open warfare.
Contest Judge
A keen-eyed scorer of bouts, races, and trials of skill at fairs and tournaments.
Lighthouse Keeper
A wick-trimming solitary keeping the coastal beacon burning for ships at sea.
Harbor Master
A berth-assigning authority logging arrivals and keeping order among docked vessels.
Ale Conner
A foam-lipped taster inspecting every batch of ale brewed in the district for quality.
Quarantine Officer
A door-sealing enforcer turning back travelers and locking plague houses shut.
Public Health Officer
A well-inspecting official keeping disease out of the community through sanitation checks.
Water Inspector
A sample-drawing tester of wells and cisterns for cleanliness and contamination.
Food Inspector
A meat-sniffing condemner of spoiled goods before they reach the buyer's table.
Market Inspector
A scale-checking walker of the marketplace, catching short measures and mislabeled wares.
Quality Controller
A guild-standard examiner inspecting finished goods before they can be sold.
Standards Keeper
A vault-dwelling guardian of the official weights, measures, and yardsticks.
Weights and Measures
A calibration-obsessed certifier of every scale and bushel used in trade.
Fair Trade Monitor
A sharp-eyed watcher for rigged dice, false bottoms, and crooked dealings at the fair.
Price Setter
A bread-and-grain regulator fixing maximum prices on essentials to prevent gouging.
